Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

Please create an outline for the Sprint Review presentation in Markdown format, saved to the day10/ directory. Include the agenda, each team member's presentation module, and Q&A time slots.

arguments.content:
# Sprint 8 — Week 1 Sprint Review (Presentation Outline)

- **Review date**: 2026-03-27 (Fri)
- **Sprint week**: Week 1 (2026-03-23 to 2026-03-27)
- **Audience**: PM + Engineering + Stakeholders

## 1) Agenda (Suggested 45–60 min)

| Time (min) | Segment | Owner | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 0–5 | Welcome, goals, and agenda | **Alex (Tech Lead)** | Frame outcomes, recap sprint goals |
| 5–10 | Week 1 highlights (what shipped / what changed) | **Alex** | Top 3–5 headline items |
| 10–20 | Backend module | **Bai (Backend)** | Demos + metrics + risks |
| 20–30 | Frontend module | **Chen (Frontend)** | Demos + UX changes + follow-ups |
| 30–38 | QA / quality module | **Dana (QA)** | Test results, defects, release readiness |
| 38–45 | Cross-team risks & blockers review | **Alex** | Decisions needed + mitigation plan |
| 45–55 | Q&A | **All** | Stakeholder questions |
| 55–60 | Wrap-up + next week preview | **Alex** | Commitments + next milestones |

## 2) Presentation Modules (By Team Member)

### Alex — Tech Lead Module (10–12 min total)

**Purpose**: Set context, ensure alignment on progress vs. sprint goals, and drive decisions.

- Sprint goal reminder (Sprint 8)
- What we committed for Week 1 vs. what we achieved
- Key architecture / platform updates (if any)
- Cross-team dependencies (PM, design, infra)
- Decisions needed today (if any)
- Next week (Week 2) focus areas

**Slide/Demo suggestions**
- 1 slide: Week 1 goals vs. actuals
- 1 slide: Major decisions + impacts

---

### Bai — Backend Module (10 min)

**Purpose**: Show backend deliverables, reliability improvements, and service health.

- Completed backend tasks (top items)
- API changes (new endpoints / deprecations / versioning updates)
- Performance / reliability metrics (p95 latency, error rate, throughput)
- Deployment notes (what was released, rollback status if any)
- Known issues and planned fixes

**Demo suggestions**
- Quick API demo / Postman collection walkthrough
- Before/after metric screenshot (if available)

---

### Chen — Frontend Module (10 min)

**Purpose**: Demonstrate user-facing changes and usability improvements.

- Completed UI features / enhancements
- UX fixes and polish items
- Any feature flags / rollout status
- Frontend performance observations (load times, bundle size notes)
- Follow-ups for next week

**Demo suggestions**
- 1–2 guided user flows showing the new behavior

---

### Dana — QA / Quality Module (8–10 min)

**Purpose**: Confirm quality posture and readiness; summarize test coverage and defects.

- Test execution summary (automated + manual)
- Coverage / quality signals (coverage %, flaky tests, build stability)
- Defect summary (new, reopened, resolved; severity)
- Release readiness recommendation
- Top quality risks and mitigations

**Artifacts to reference**
- Latest CI build report
- Test coverage report
- Error event summary

## 3) Q&A (10 min)

| Slot | Time (min) | Facilitation | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Q&A #1 (clarifying questions) | 5 | Alex | Focus on scope and delivered outcomes |
| Q&A #2 (risks / roadmap) | 5 | Alex + Owners | Confirm next steps and owners |

## 4) Close (5 min)

- Recap key takeaways
- Confirm decisions and owners
- Confirm next-week deliverables and review checkpoints
